title: melaleuca buseana
intro: Melaleuca buseana, also known as the bushy paperbark, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family that is native to Australia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Melaleuca buseana include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and insect bites, as well as its potential to support respiratory health and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea infusions and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
